items.prompt: How should AI-assisted labeling treat model proposals before they enter the training pool?
choices: Humans correct errors; strict confidence thresholds and audits apply
Skip QC because the model is faster
items.explain: Human correction and audit sampling gate self-labeling and pseudo-labels.

items.prompt: What is required before deploying synthetic labels for rare classes?
choices: Validation against real distributions and gold evaluation sets
items.explain: Synthetic data must be checked against real-world distributions.

items.prompt: Real-time annotation for safety-critical paths should still keep human review for schema changes and edge cases.
items.explain: Streaming speed does not remove governance on critical decisions.

items.prompt: What does Chapter 5 address after annotation, according to this clip?
items.rubric: Cleaning and preprocessing to make labeled datasets model-ready.
items.sample_answer: Chapter 5 covers cleaning and preprocessing so labeled datasets become model-ready tables and tensors.

items.prompt: Weak supervision and labeling functions are best described as:
choices: Heuristics humans audit, with gold evaluation still required
items.explain: Programmatic labels bootstrap work but need gold evaluation and audit.
